claim: The Currier A/B split reflects a genuine linguistic difference between two distinct languages or dialects.
evidence_for: Currier A/B split is significant (R-squared=0.9084) and moderate Zipf fit
evidence_against: Glyph positional constraints do not differ significantly between the two languages
test: Compare word and glyph distributions between Currier A and B using a chi-squared test

tests_run.test: compare word and glyph distributions between currier a and b using a chi-squared test
tests_run.details: Language A: entropy=3.8321, 3513 unique/11022 total words. Language B: entropy=3.8611, 5089 unique/23766 total words. Entropy difference: 0.0290. Vocabulary density difference: 0.1046.
